nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving or still freshwater habitats — streams, rivers, and shallow pools in tropical Southeast Asia, where it grows as a true aquatic Cyperaceae rather than the more common emersed-only sedges

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich aquasoil or fine-grained planted-tank substrate
open background planting space allowing long leaves to sway

goodWith: nature-style and Southeast Asian biotope aquascapes
avoidWith: large fish that uproot rosette plants

summary: Cyperus helferi is a Thai sedge with long, grass-like flowing leaves grown as a background rosette plant. It is one of the few true Cyperaceae that grows reliably submerged, generally advised with nutrient-rich substrate and medium-to-high light.
careNotes: Cyperus helferi is a Southeast Asian sedge (family Cyperaceae, order Poales) native to Thailand and adjacent regions, and is one of the few members of its family that grows reliably underwater rather than only emersed. It forms a distinctive grass-like rosette of long, narrow, ribbon-like leaves that sway beautifully in moving water, and is typically used as a background plant where it commonly reaches 20–50 cm in height with a rosette diameter of around 15–25 cm. Sources differ on care level: Tropica rates it medium, Buce Plant rates it easy, and The Aquarium Guide considers it unsuitable for beginners; the central averaged position is intermediate. Lighting is commonly described as medium to high (Tropica ~0.5 W/L; The Aquarium Guide recommends roughly 2–4 W/gallon or ~120–180 lumens/gallon equivalent), and CO2 supplementation is generally advised (Tropica 6–14 mg/L) to encourage steady growth — without it growth is even slower than its already-slow baseline. Temperature in most aquarium-friendly sources falls in the 22–28 °C band (Buce Plant advises keeping water below ~27 °C / 80 °F; The Aquarium Guide gives an ideal 25–27 °C within a tolerated 20–30 °C span). pH is generally tolerated from about 6.0 to 7.5 with a slight preference for soft, slightly acidic water; gH 1–15 is the centered span from a wider 1–20 °dH tolerance reported in care guides. Cyperus helferi is a heavy root feeder and benefits strongly from a fine-grained, nutrient-rich planted-tank substrate (aquasoil or similar) supplemented with root tabs; comprehensive water-column fertilization (NPK plus micronutrients including iron) is also generally helpful. Growth is slow — sources note it can take many months to reach full height. Despite being a heavy root feeder, its root structure is relatively delicate, so avoid strong current and avoid large fish that uproot plants. Plant single rosettes into the substrate with care; trim damaged outer leaves to encourage fresh growth.
breedingNotes: Propagation is vegetative: mature plants develop adventitious daughter plantlets at the base of the mother rosette, which can be separated once they have their own root systems and replanted. Tip cuttings replanted into nutrient-rich substrate are also documented but less common than dividing daughter plants.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Plant details page resolved from Tropica search for Cyperus helferi. Provided height (20–30+ cm), rosette diameter (15–25 cm), medium light demand (~0.5 W/L), medium CO2 (6–14 mg/L), slow growth rate, Asian/Thai origin, and medium difficulty.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Cyperaceae), height (8–20+ in / ~20–50 cm), pH 6.0–7.5, recommended temperature below ~27 °C / 80 °F, medium-to-high light, slow growth, easy care rating, background placement, and propagation by cutting new growth with attached roots.

additional.name: The Aquarium Guide
additional.notes: Used for temperature range (20–30 °C, ideal 25–27 °C), pH 5–7.5, hardness 1–20 °dH, lighting (2–4 W/gal), CO2 recommendation, nutrient-rich substrate and NPK+micronutrient fertilization guidance, ~100 L (≈ 20 gal) minimum tank size, adventitious daughter-plant propagation, and a more cautious 'intermediate-to-advanced' care assessment. Care level averaged across the three sources to 'intermediate'; pH range centered to 6.0–7.5; gH centered to 1–15.
